reasoning: Restates WIN-054 (El Gordo cluster velocity >2,500 km/s inconsistent with ΛCDM). WIN-054 is verdicted Not Demonstrated: the dome claims El Gordo's infall velocity is impossible under ΛCDM, but this relies on a 2012 paper (Menanteau et al.) whose velocity estimate has been superseded. The Donnert (2014) and Zhang et al. (2015) hydrodynamic simulations show infall velocities of ~2,000-2,500 km/s are achievable within ΛCDM at low probability but not impossibility. The dome registered this as PRED-082 on 2026-04-04, but El Gordo data has been public since 2012 — this is a textbook post-hoc restatement of an existing claim that we have already reviewed.
kernel_of_truth: El Gordo IS genuinely anomalous — its collision velocity challenges ΛCDM expectations. The tension is real and discussed in the literature. But framing a known anomaly as a dome prediction when the data predates registration by 14 years is not predictive science.
